definition: Monetary transmission has eventually worked with a 100% base rate across five post-1985 cycles (1994-95, 1999-2000, 2004-07, 2015-18, 2022-25 in progress), with lags ranging from 18 to 24+ months. Every cycle featured credible arguments that transmission was broken; it always arrived through unexpected channels. The current 36-month lag with +0.3pp unemployment is within historical variance.
evidence: Every cycle had credible broken-transmission arguments
Transmission arrived through unexpected channels each time
open_questions: What unemployment rate at what lag would confirm genuinely broken transmission?
Has private credit at scale, AI wealth effects, or mortgage lock-in genuinely impaired channels?
Is the current cycle within historical variance or a structural break?
